question.text: Under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008, what is the compliance officer required to report to the board of directors?

answer.text: Any material non-compliance by the intermediary, in writing
Daily trading volumes of the intermediary
Client acquisition data on a quarterly basis

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The compliance officer shall report to the intermediary or its board of directors in writing of any material non-compliance by the intermediary. **The Trap:** Financial and operational reports are important but not the specific reporting mandate of the compliance officer under these regulations. **Cross-Exam Context:** Compliance officer duties are tested in NISM Series III-A and III-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The keyword is 'material' non-compliance - the compliance officer must exercise judgment on what rises to the level of board reporting.
